mnemonics: VIOLATE Rules Cause Problems: Visualize Integrity, Options, Levels, Actions, Types, Errors. Rules, Constraints, Prevention.
keyPoints: A constraint violation occurs when an operation (INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE) breaks a defined database rule like a PRIMARY KEY, FOREIGN KEY, UNIQUE, CHECK, or NOT NULL constraint.
The DBMS can be configured to handle violations by either rejecting the operation (RESTRICT/NO ACTION) or performing a corrective action (CASCADE, SET NULL, SET DEFAULT).
Proper handling is crucial for maintaining data integrity; the chosen action depends on the business logic and the relationship between the data.
commonMistakes: Assuming CASCADE is always the best option, which can lead to unintended massive deletions and loss of data.
Forgetting that a DBMS will automatically enforce constraints, leading to unexpected transaction rollbacks or errors if not handled in application code.
Creating circular dependencies with foreign key actions, e.g., where a CASCADE update on one table triggers a CASCADE update on another that re-triggers the first, causing an error.
